title: Ostrumite Aegis
contents: text | A rune-carved Ostrumite crystal is embedded at the shield’s center. The crystal absorbs kinetic force and converts it into spectral energy that can be released to protect its bearer and nearby allies.
description | Kinetic Absorption | When you take damage from a creature you can see while wielding this shield, the aegis gains 1 charge (maximum 1 per turn). The shield can hold up to 5 charges.
description | Stored Deflection | When you take damage, you may use your reaction to expend 1 charge and reduce the damage by 1d8.
description | Spirit Guard | When a creature within 10 feet of you takes damage, you may use your reaction and expend 2 charges to reduce that damage by 1d10 + your proficiency bonus.
description | Kinetic Rebound | When a creature hits you with a melee attack, you may expend 3 charges to deal 2d8 force damage to that creature.

title: Crumb, the Watchful Crow
contents: property | Senses | Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 16
description | Bonded Companion | Crumb acts on your initiative and obeys simple verbal commands without requiring an action.
description | Rogue’s Assistant | While Crumb is within 30 feet of you, once per short rest you gain advantage on a Dexterity (Stealth) or Wisdom (Perception) check.
description | Harassing Strike | As a bonus action, Crumb can harass a creature within 5 feet. The next attack roll made against that creature before the end of your turn has advantage.
description | Aerial Carry | Crumb can carry or assist a Small or Tiny creature while flying for short distances. While carrying a creature, his fly speed becomes 30 ft.
description | Eyes of the Crow | Crumb can scout up to 500 feet away. As an action you may see and hear through his senses until the start of your next turn.
description | Talons | +5 to hit, 1d6 + 3 slashing damage.
description | Blinding Peck (Recharge 5–6) | Crumb pecks at a creature's eyes. Target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be blinded until the start of its next turn.

title: Bracer of Flying Daggers
contents: text | This leather forearm bracer contains several narrow rune-cut slots holding thin throwing daggers formed of shimmering arcane force. Though only a few appear at once, the bracer never truly runs empty.
description | Arcane Daggers | As an action, you may pull up to two magical daggers from the bracer and immediately throw them, making a separate ranged attack with each dagger.

description | Magical Weapon | Each dagger has a range of 20/60 ft., deals 1d4 + your Dexterity modifier piercing damage, counts as magical, and disappears immediately after the attack hits or misses.
description | Limited Volley | The bracer can produce up to 10 daggers per long rest. Throwing one dagger expends 1 charge. Throwing two daggers expends 2 charges.

title: Blinkstep Dagger
contents: text | This slender dagger contains a sliver of rune-cut Ostrumite along its spine. When thrown or thrust forward, the crystal briefly fractures space itself, pulling the wielder through the dagger’s path in a flash of violet light.

description | Blinkstep | Immediately after you make an attack with this dagger (hit or miss), you may expend 1 charge to teleport to an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the target or the dagger’s landing point.

description | Recall Flash | If you throw the dagger, you may expend 1 charge to teleport to the dagger’s location instead of having it fall normally. The dagger then instantly returns to your hand after the teleport completes.
description | Phase Withdrawal | As a bonus action, you may expend 2 charges to teleport up to 15 feet to a space you can see.
description | Magical Weapon | This dagger counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks.

title: Mask of the Dead Star
contents: text | This pitch-black mask devours light, its surface reflecting nothing.
description | Event Horizon | Light is subtly absorbed in a 5-foot radius around you. The space you occupy is considered one light level dimmer than its surroundings.

description | Gravitic Drain | As an action, you tap into the ravenous hunger of a dead star, draining the life force of nearby creatures. Each living creature of your choice within 15 feet takes 1d4 necrotic damage. For each creature damaged this way, you may either regain 1d4 hit points or recover one expended spell slot. Recovering spell slots of 2nd level or higher requires one additional affected creature per spell level above 1st.

title: Spellbook of Dunamancy
contents: text | This ancient spellbook hums with gravitational tension, its ink drifting across the pages.
description | Arcane Focus | This spellbook functions as both a spellbook and an arcane focus.

description | Forbidden Casting | Once per long rest, you may attempt to cast a spell from this book even if you cannot normally cast spells of that level. For each spell level above your highest available spell slot, you take 1d4 necrotic damage and gain 1 level of exhaustion (maximum of 5).
description | Included Spells | 4th: Spirit of Death, Gravity Sinkhole. 5th: Negative Energy Flood, Wall of Light. 6th: Gravity Fissure. 7th: Tether Essence.

title: Ostrumite Gunblade (Prototype)
contents: property | Properties | Versatile (1d10), Special, Ostrumite, Ammunition
property | Type | Martial Weapon, Hybrid Weapon
description | Hybrid Weapon | The Ostrumite Gunblade functions as both a melee weapon and a firearm. You may choose which mode to use each time you make an attack.

description | Ostrumite Core (Prototype) | When you roll a 1–3 on an attack with the firearm mode, roll a d6. On a 1–2, the weapon misfires and the firearm mode cannot be used until the end of your next turn. On a 3–6, no additional effect occurs.
text | The blade hums softly, vibrating against the hilt. When fired, the sound resembles a thunderclap trapped inside steel.

title: Ring of Protection
contents: description | Protective Ward | While wearing this ring, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

title: Quick Lunar Ring
contents: description | Lunar Agility | While it is night or you are under moonlight, you gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and your walking speed increases by 5 feet.

title: Ring of Spell Storing
contents: description | Stored Spell | This ring can store a single spell of 1st, 2nd, or 3rd level cast into it. While wearing the ring, you can cast the stored spell without expending a spell slot. The spell uses the original caster’s spellcasting ability.

title: Quiver of the Unerring Hunt
contents: description | Tracking Arrows | Arrows drawn from this quiver subtly adjust their trajectory. When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using ammunition from this quiver, you have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track that creature for the next 24 hours.
description | Unerring Path | Once per long rest, when you miss a ranged weapon attack using ammunition from this quiver, you may reroll the attack roll and use the new result.

title: Violin of Whispering Groves
contents: text | This finely carved violin resonates with the quiet music of forests and hidden glades, its tone carrying the breath of moss, wind, and watchful spirits.
description | Instrument of the Bards | A creature that attempts to play the violin without being attuned to it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 psychic damage.

description | Nature’s Repertoire | You can use an action to play the violin and cast one of the following spells from it: Entangle, Faerie Fire, Shillelagh, or Speak with Animals. Once the violin has been used to cast a spell, it can’t be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. These spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

description | Beguiling Melody | You can play the violin while casting a spell that causes one or more of its targets to be charmed on a failed saving throw, imposing disadvantage on the saving throw. This benefit applies only if the spell has a somatic or material component.

title: Queen's Banner
contents: property | Attunement | Required (creature proficient with shields or martial weapons)

text | This heavy battlefield banner bears the royal crest of the crown. Its cloth is reinforced with metal threads so it can be driven into stone or soil alike, marking the place where the Queen's soldiers stand their ground.
description | Plant the Banner | As an action, you drive the banner into the ground in a space within 5 feet of you. The banner remains planted until removed and emits a 10-foot aura of resolve.

description | Aura of Resolve | Friendly creatures of your choice that start their turn in the aura gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier. A creature can gain these temporary hit points only once per round.
description | Hold the Line | While you are within the banner’s aura, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
description | Remove the Banner | You can pull the banner free as a bonus action. An enemy creature within 5 feet can use an action to attempt to wrench it free with a DC 13 Strength check.
description | Uses | The banner can be planted a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.

title: Lunar Crown
contents: text | This adaptive crown reshapes itself to suit its wearer, forming a circlet, diadem, or crown-like band according to personal taste. No matter its form, a crescent moon of polished purple Ostrumite ore rests at its center, glowing softly with lunar light.
description | Lunar Amplification | When you cast a spell that restores hit points or removes conditions, you may expend 1 charge to empower the spell.

description | Direct Lunar Upgrade | If the spell has a direct, higher-level version (such as cure wounds becoming mass cure wounds, or lesser restoration becoming greater restoration), the spell is instead cast as that higher-level spell.
description | Arcane Scaling | If the spell does not have a direct upgraded version, it is cast as if using a spell slot one level higher than the slot you expend.

description | Casting Limits | This effect does not grant access to spells you do not know or have prepared. Any additional material components, casting time changes, or other requirements of the upgraded spell must be met. The spell cannot be enhanced beyond the highest spell level you are capable of casting.
